title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A Go pairing room incrementally implements a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model, first with deterministic values and then with a changed failure, while keeping prior messaging invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go messaging team handles case 3147 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dual Write Failure Matrix.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence. For RabbitMQ and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dual Write Failure Matrix, keep Dual Write Failure Matrix connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-saga-compensation-1cmipx" that uses case 1347 to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.
